channel.title: OOIR Trends: Clinical Medicine (Rheumatology)
channel.description: Aktuelle Paper-Trends im Bereich Clinical Medicine (Rheumatology) von OOIR (mit Titel und Metadaten von Crossref)

item.title: The 2026 British Society for Rheumatology guideline for the management of children, young people and adults with systemic lupus erythematosus
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Rheumatology Score: 75 Autoren: Md Yuzaiful Md Yusof, Eve M D Smith, Hanna Lythgoe, Antonios Psarras, Kate Armon, Michael W Beresford, Lindsey Cherry, Nadia Corp, Christopher J Edwards, Lambert Felix, Kalveer Flora, Rebecca Gilman, Bridget Griffiths, Caroline Gordon, David Isenberg, Natasha Jordan, Debbie Kinsey, Arvind Kaul, Philip M Laws, Liz Lightstone, Christian D Mallen, Stephen D Marks, Naomi Maxwell, Zoe McLaren, Elena Moraitis, Clare Nash, Ruth J Pepper, Clarissa Pilkington, Heather Rostron, Jade Skeates, Sarah Skeoch, Dalila Tremarias, Danielle A van der Windt, Chris Wincup, Asad Zoma, Peter Lanyon, Edward M Vital, British Society for Rheumatology Guidelines Steering Group members, Edward Roddy, Devesh Mewar, Arvind Kaur, Coziana Ciurtin, Sandrine Lacassagne, Anoop Kuttikat, Emma Williams, Abhishek Abhishek, Christopher Joyce, Karen Merrison, Emmandeep Dhillon, Claire Jones, Emily Rose-Parfitt, Hirushi Jayasekera, Pratyasha Saha Journal: Rheumatology Veröffentlicht: 2026-06-01 Abstract: Abstract Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) is a lifelong autoimmune condition with multi-system involvement that is associated with morbidity, mortality, and poor quality of life. The key aim of management should be to empower individuals with SLE to manage their condition, suppressing systemic disease activity, and preventing organ damage. This guideline builds on and expands the recommendations developed for the first guideline published in 2017 for adults living with SLE. This comprehensive life-course guideline was developed according to the British Society for Rheumatology Guidelines Protocol by a Guideline Working Group (GWG) comprising healthcare professionals with expertise in paediatric and adult SLE, and people with lived experience. The GWG reviewed published guidelines, undertook a systematic literature review and utilised expertise from specialist lupus centres across the UK and patient representatives to formulate a list of 102 recommendations with corresponding strength of recommendations and agreement scores. These recommendations encompass advances in the assessment, diagnosis, monitoring, general approaches to management, non-pharmacological and pharmacological management of SLE, organ-specific treatment including lupus nephritis and cutaneous lupus erythematosus, as well as the delivery of care in the context of the UK healthcare system. Furthermore, we have provided research and audit recommendations to support equitable access to care and improve health outcomes in SLE. DOI: 10.1093/rheumatology/keag223 ISSN: 1462-0324 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-19

item.title: TLR7 in systemic lupus erythematosus: genetics and emerging therapies
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Rheumatology Score: 28 Autoren: Carola G. Vinuesa, Madhumita Shrotri, Anisur Rahman Journal: Nature Reviews Rheumatology Veröffentlicht: 2026-06-19 DOI: 10.1038/s41584-026-01388-0 ISSN: 1759-4790 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-19

item.title: The end for avacopan

item.title: Predictors of unacceptable pain and the impact of sociodemographic factors and comorbidities—results from a large, nationwide cohort of early rheumatoid arthritis
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Rheumatology Score: 24 Autoren: Anna Eberhard, Daniela Di Giuseppe, Stefan Bergman, Jon Lampa, Thomas Mandl, Tor Olofsson, Kristin Waldenlind, Johan Askling, Carl Turesson Journal: Annals of the Rheumatic Diseases Veröffentlicht: 2026-06-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.ard.2026.05.028 ISSN: 0003-4967 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-19

item.title: Mapping inequalities in rheumatology care in Europe: the first edition of the EULAR RheumaFacts project
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Rheumatology Score: 23 Autoren: Anna Moltó, Anastasiia Miendrova, Laure Gossec, Loreto Carmona, Konstantinos Billis, Coralie Signorell, Zoltán Szekanecz, Jose-Maria Alvaro-Gracia, Anja Strangfeld, Margreet Kloppenburg, Nina Brodin, Thijs Willem Swinnen, Peter Böhm, Mariana Carvalho, Dirk Foell, Artur Kollcaku, Valentina Vardanyan, Ruth Fritsch-Stork, Philip Remans, Nikolay Stoilov, Branimir Anić, Nikolas Malliotis, Kristýna Bubová, Sandra Meisalu, Pia Isomäki, Johanna Callhoff, Maia Tsutskiridze, Peter Balint, John Ryan, Amit Druyan, Fabrizio Conti, Nelly Ziade, Dalia Miltiniene, Elena Deseatnicova, Ana Bulatovic, Astrid van Tubergen, Ljubinka Damjanovska Krstikj, Gunnstein Bakland, Mariusz Korkosz, Patricia Pinto, Elena Rezus, Predrag Ostojic, Lenka Tarabčáková, Ziga Rotar, Chamaida Plasencia-Rodriguez, Jon Thorkell Einarsson, Daniel Aeberli, Emre Bilgin, İsmihan Sunar, Olena Garmish, Gary J. Macfarlane, Sofia Ramiro, Annelies Boonen Journal: Annals of the Rheumatic Diseases Veröffentlicht: 2026-06-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.ard.2026.05.032 ISSN: 0003-4967 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-19
